stimulus:
The following text is from the 1922 poem “Harvest Round” by Lola Ridge, an Irish-American poet.

There shall be other harvests rising,
A new turning of the wheel—
Our hands shall have a different gleaning,
Pale fields renew their grain—
But under the new moon’s meaning,
Repeating what dusk began,
There’ll be the same shoulders aching,
The same tired hands shall reel—
When this dusk has done its breaking
And dawn shall come again.....
stem: Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?

answerOptions.content: To question whether tasks completed at one time of day are more meaningful than those completed at another time
To consider how the cyclical nature of human labor can be both renewing and burdensome
To refute the idea that hopeful feelings are more commonly experienced than weary ones
To demonstrate how the experiences of farmworkers relate to the experiences of their communities

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer because it accurately states the main purpose of the text. The text begins with images of renewal—“other harvests rising,” “a new turning of the wheel,” “pale fields renew their grain”—promising change. But the poem then notes that under the “new moon’s meaning” there will be “the same shoulders aching” and “the same tired hands.” The poem thus considers both the renewing and the burdensome aspects of cyclical human labor.

Choice A is incorrect because the text does not compare meaningfulness of tasks at different times. Choice C is incorrect because the text does not compare the frequency of hopeful and weary feelings. Choice D is incorrect because the text does not distinguish between individuals and communities.
